table.description: Tables (also known as hash tables or dictionaries) are collections of key/value pairs. They are defined by headers, with square brackets on a line by themselves. All keys and their values beneath a header belong to that table until another header or EOF.

array_of_tables.description: An array of tables can be expressed by using a header with double brackets. Each header with the same name defines an element in the array of tables. The tables are inserted in the order encountered.

key_value.description: A key/value pair is the primary building block of a TOML document. Keys are on the left of the equals sign and values are on the right. Whitespace around the equals sign is ignored.

dotted_key.description: Dotted keys are a sequence of bare or quoted keys joined with a dot. This allows for grouping similar properties together without requiring separate table headers.

inline_table.description: Inline tables provide a more compact syntax for expressing tables. They are intended to appear on a single line. No newlines are allowed between the curly braces unless they are valid within a value.

comment.description: A hash symbol (#) marks the rest of the line as a comment, except when inside a string. Comments can appear on their own line or at the end of a line with a key-value pair.

string_basic.description: Basic strings are surrounded by quotation marks ("). Any Unicode character may be used except those that must be escaped: quotation mark, backslash, and the control characters (U+0000 to U+001F, U+007F).

string_literal.description: Literal strings are surrounded by single quotes. What you see is what you get — there is no escaping. Because there is no escaping, there is no way to write a single quote inside a literal string.

string_multiline_basic.description: Multi-line basic strings are surrounded on each side by three quotation marks and allow newlines. A newline immediately following the opening delimiter will be trimmed. All other content between the delimiters is interpreted as-is without modification.

string_multiline_literal.description: Multi-line literal strings are surrounded on each side by three single quotes and allow newlines. No escaping whatsoever is performed. A newline immediately following the opening delimiter will be trimmed.

integer.description: Integers are whole numbers. Positive numbers may be prefixed with a plus sign. Leading zeros are not allowed. Hex (0x), octal (0o), and binary (0b) representations are supported. Underscores between digits aid readability.

float.description: Floats should be implemented as IEEE 754 64-bit values. A float consists of an integer part followed by a fractional part and/or an exponent part. Special float values inf and nan are supported.

boolean.description: Booleans are the tokens true or false, always lowercase.

offset_datetime.description: An offset date-time represents a specific instant in time, specified as a full date, a T delimiter (or space), a full time, and a timezone offset. Conforms to RFC 3339.

local_datetime.description: A local date-time omits the timezone offset. It represents a date-time without relation to any specific timezone or offset. Cannot be converted to an instant in time without additional information.

local_date.description: A local date represents the date portion of an RFC 3339 formatted date-time without the time component. It represents a whole day without time or timezone.

local_time.description: A local time represents the time portion of an RFC 3339 formatted date-time without the date or timezone. Millisecond precision is supported.

array.description: Arrays are square brackets with values inside, separated by commas. Arrays can span multiple lines. A trailing comma after the last value is permitted. Arrays can contain mixed types.

cargo_package.description: Defines Rust package metadata in Cargo.toml. Contains fields like name, version, edition, description, license, authors, repository, documentation, homepage, and readme.

cargo_dependencies.description: Specifies the runtime dependencies of a Rust crate in Cargo.toml. Each entry maps a crate name to a version requirement, which can be a simple string or a table with version, features, optional, git, path, etc.

cargo_dev_dependencies.description: Specifies dependencies only needed for development in Cargo.toml. These are used for compiling tests, examples, and benchmarks but are not propagated to dependent crates.

cargo_build_dependencies.description: Specifies dependencies needed by build scripts (build.rs) in Cargo.toml. These are compiled and made available to the build script before the main crate is compiled.

cargo_features.description: Defines conditional compilation features for a Rust crate in Cargo.toml. Features can enable optional dependencies or other features. A 'default' key specifies features enabled by default.

cargo_workspace.description: Defines a Cargo workspace for managing multiple related packages. Contains members (list of package paths), exclude, default-members, and resolver fields.

cargo_lib.description: Configures the library target in Cargo.toml. Fields include name, path, crate-type (lib, cdylib, staticlib, etc.), and doc/test/bench toggles.

cargo_bin.description: Defines a binary target in Cargo.toml. Each [[bin]] entry specifies a separate binary with name, path, and optional required-features.

cargo_profile_release.description: Customizes the release build profile in Cargo.toml. Common settings include opt-level, lto, codegen-units, panic, strip, and debug.

cargo_profile_dev.description: Customizes the development build profile in Cargo.toml. The default profile used by `cargo build` and `cargo run`. Common settings include opt-level and debug.

pyproject_build_system.description: Specifies the build system for a Python project in pyproject.toml, as defined by PEP 517/518. Contains 'requires' (list of build dependencies) and 'build-backend' (the build backend module).

pyproject_project.description: Declares core Python project metadata in pyproject.toml as defined by PEP 621. Fields include name, version, description, requires-python, license, authors, dependencies, classifiers, urls, scripts, and entry-points.

pyproject_optional_dependencies.description: Defines optional dependency groups (extras) for a Python project. Users install them with `pip install package[extra]`. Each key maps a group name to a list of dependency specifiers.

tool_pytest.description: Configures Pytest within pyproject.toml. Supports testpaths, python_files, python_classes, python_functions, addopts, markers, filterwarnings, and more.

tool_black.description: Configures the Black code formatter within pyproject.toml. Options include line-length, target-version, include/exclude patterns, and skip-string-normalization.

tool_ruff.description: Configures the Ruff linter within pyproject.toml. Options include line-length, target-version, select/ignore rules, fixable/unfixable rules, and per-file overrides.

tool_mypy.description: Configures the Mypy static type checker within pyproject.toml. Options include python_version, strict, warn_return_any, disallow_untyped_defs, and per-module overrides.

tool_setuptools.description: Configures setuptools within pyproject.toml for package discovery, package data, and dynamic versioning.

tool_poetry.description: Configures a Poetry-managed Python project within pyproject.toml. Includes name, version, description, authors, dependencies, dev-dependencies, scripts, and plugins.

server_config.description: Common server configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes host, port, TLS/SSL settings, timeouts, and worker counts.

database_config.description: Common database configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes connection URL, pool size, max idle connections, and timeout settings.

logging_config.description: Common logging configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes log level, output file path, format (json/text), and rotation settings.

auth_config.description: Common authentication configuration pattern in TOML config files. Typically includes JWT secret key, token expiry duration, refresh token settings, and issuer.
